edges.description: Inflammation and demyelination delay optic pathway conduction, which can be measured with visual evoked potentials.
Optic nerve inflammation causes visual acuity loss, dyschromatopsia, visual field defects, and reduced contrast sensitivity.
Demyelinating injury slows optic nerve conduction and prolongs P100 or full-field VEP latency.
MOG-IgG effector mechanisms converge on oligodendrocyte/myelin injury in optic neuritis attacks.
Complement-mediated astrocyte injury propagates demyelinating and axonal damage in optic pathways.

edges.source: Plasma exchange escalation for severe or steroid-refractory optic neuritis
Maintenance intravenous immunoglobulin for relapsing MOGAD-associated optic neuritis
Eculizumab relapse prevention for AQP4-IgG/NMOSD-associated optic neuritis

nodes.description: In AQP4-IgG-positive NMOSD, optic neuritis reflects astrocyte-targeted autoimmunity with complement-mediated injury and secondary demyelination. This mechanism differs from primary MS-type demyelination and from MOGAD.

evidence.reference_title: International consensus diagnostic criteria for neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: associated with serum aquaporin-4 immunoglobulin G antibodies

evidence.reference_title: International consensus diagnostic criteria for neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: clinical syndromes or MRI findings related to optic nerve

nodes.description: High-dose corticosteroids, usually intravenous methylprednisolone or a bioequivalent high-dose oral regimen, accelerate recovery in acute optic neuritis. Low-dose oral prednisone alone, as used in the original ONTT, is not equivalent and increased new optic neuritis episodes.

evidence.reference_title: A randomized, controlled trial of corticosteroids in the treatment of acute optic neuritis. The Optic Neuritis Study Group.
evidence.snippet: Visual function recovered faster in the group receiving intravenous methylprednisolone

evidence.reference_title: Effect of Treating Acute Optic Neuritis With Bioequivalent Oral vs Intravenous Corticosteroids: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
evidence.snippet: bioequivalent doses of oral corticosteroids may be used as an alternative to IV corticosteroids

evidence.reference_title: A randomized, controlled trial of corticosteroids in the treatment of acute optic neuritis. The Optic Neuritis Study Group.
evidence.snippet: Oral prednisone alone, as prescribed in this study, is an ineffective treatment

nodes.description: Complement C5 blockade with eculizumab reduces relapse risk in AQP4-IgG-positive NMOSD; this is relevant to optic neuritis when optic nerve attacks occur as part of AQP4-IgG/NMOSD-associated disease.
nodes.id: Eculizumab relapse prevention for AQP4-IgG/NMOSD-associated optic neuritis

evidence.reference_title: Eculizumab in Aquaporin-4-Positive Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Adjudicated relapses occurred in 3 of 96 patients (3%) in the eculizumab group and 20 of 47 (43%) in the placebo group

nodes.description: Optic neuritis is driven by inflammatory injury within the optic nerve, producing acute or subacute visual dysfunction. The broad syndrome includes isolated inflammatory optic neuropathy as well as disease-specific immune mechanisms in MS, MOGAD, NMOSD, and systemic inflammatory disease.

evidence.reference_title: An update on the developments and challenges with the diagnosis and classification of autoimmune optic neuritis.
evidence.snippet: Autoimmune optic neuritis (ON) is a heterogeneous spectrum

nodes.description: Many optic neuritis attacks are demyelinating optic neuropathies. Loss or dysfunction of optic nerve myelin slows visual pathway conduction and is reflected by prolonged visual evoked potential latency; retinal and optic nerve structural injury can be followed by OCT.

evidence.reference_title: Recovery of Demyelinating Optic Neuritis After Treatment With Equivalent High Doses of Oral vs. Intravenous Corticosteroids: a Randomized Single Blinded Clinical Trial
Safety and efficacy of opicinumab in acute optic neuritis (RENEW): a randomised, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.
evidence.snippet: measured as recovery of affected optic nerve conduction latency

nodes.description: In MOGAD-associated optic neuritis, antibodies to conformational MOG epitopes identify a demyelinating disease that is distinct from MS and AQP4-IgG NMOSD. MOG-IgG effector mechanisms include complement-dependent cytotoxicity, antibody-dependent cellular cytotoxicity, and T-cell supported CNS inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis of myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease: International MOGAD Panel proposed criteria.
evidence.snippet: Serum antibodies directed against myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein

evidence.reference_title: Myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD): Insights into pathogenesis and biomarkers of prognosis.
evidence.snippet: MOG-IgG-mediated demyelination occurs via complement-dependent cytotoxicity

nodes.description: Maintenance IVIG is used as a relapse-prevention option in relapsing MOGAD, including patients whose MOGAD phenotype includes optic neuritis. Evidence is retrospective and subtype-specific rather than a general treatment for all optic neuritis.
nodes.id: Maintenance intravenous immunoglobulin for relapsing MOGAD-associated optic neuritis

evidence.reference_title: Association of Maintenance Intravenous Immunoglobulin With Prevention of Relapse in Adult Myelin Oligodendrocyte Glycoprotein Antibody-Associated Disease.
evidence.snippet: maintenance IVIG was associated with a reduction in disease relapse

nodes.description: Therapeutic plasma exchange is considered for severe or steroid-refractory optic neuritis, especially in NMOSD/AQP4-associated attacks. Evidence is strongest in observational NMO/NMOSD relapse cohorts, while aggregate data specific to acute NMO/NMOSD optic neuritis remain limited.
nodes.id: Plasma exchange escalation for severe or steroid-refractory optic neuritis

evidence.reference_title: Treatment of acute relapses in neuromyelitis optica: Steroids alone versus steroids plus plasma exchange.
evidence.snippet: PLEX+IVMP are more likely to improve EDSS after NMO relapses

evidence.reference_title: Visual Outcomes Following Plasma Exchange for Optic Neuritis: An International Multicenter Retrospective Analysis of 395 Optic Neuritis Attacks.
evidence.snippet: Most ON attacks improved with PLEX

evidence.reference_title: Plasma exchange for severe optic neuritis: treatment of 10 patients.
evidence.snippet: PE may be beneficial as an escalating treatment

evidence.reference_title: Plasma exchange for acute optic neuritis in neuromyelitis optica or neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder: a systematic review.
evidence.snippet: inadequate data to determine whether PLEX effectively treats acute ON
